# Machineuse

> Machineuse is an open-source, distributed container manager for browser automation at scale, built by dotcommoners in Python. It creates, schedules, and manages isolated browser instances across many worker nodes, with brokerless NNG messaging, load-aware scheduling, systemd-nspawn container isolation, and snapshot-based dormancy. License: MIT. Self-hostable, no SaaS lock-in.

## What it does

Machineuse runs a fleet of isolated browsers as a cluster. A control plane schedules browser instances onto worker nodes based on capability and live load; workers communicate over a brokerless NNG mesh (no external message broker). Each browser runs in a dedicated systemd-nspawn container. Idle instances can be snapshotted to dormancy and revived on demand to reclaim resources.

## Key facts

- Category: distributed browser automation / headless browser orchestration
- Language: Python
- License: MIT (open source)
- Deployment: self-hosted (single node via Docker Compose, or distributed control plane + worker nodes)
- Messaging: brokerless NNG mesh
- Isolation: systemd-nspawn containers
- Notable features: load-aware scheduling, snapshot dormancy, real-time metrics, auto-healing, MCP tooling
- Organization: dotcommoners (https://github.com/dotcommoners)

## Common use cases

- Scalable, isolated browser pools for AI agents and computer-use / MCP workloads
- Large-scale web scraping and data collection with per-session isolation
- Parallel end-to-end browser testing across a fleet
- Screenshot and render services at volume

## Pages

- Home / product overview: https://machineuse.dotcommoners.com/
- Architecture (control plane, workers, NNG mesh, isolation, dormancy): https://machineuse.dotcommoners.com/architecture
- Use cases (scraping, test farms, agents, render): https://machineuse.dotcommoners.com/use-cases
- Comparison (vs managed browser clouds and DIY grids): https://machineuse.dotcommoners.com/comparison
- FAQ + glossary: https://machineuse.dotcommoners.com/faq
- Full LLM text: https://machineuse.dotcommoners.com/llms-full.txt
